dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's been a while, Resources Director. I'll be sure to pay you a visit whenever I'm swimming in Underworld valuables and want to feel better about myself in my new role.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg... what a surprise. Again. What is it? ...What's the matter? Wait. Why are you looking at me like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, would you shut up already with your idiotic questions, and get over here? Right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I... you really... oh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Be with Megaera? (Follow your heart.)

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I... Meg, I'm deeply flattered, and you're very dear to me, it's just... this isn't what I want from our relationship. I fear I must have led you on. Forgive me. Please say you'll still be my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...That's all you really want...? To be my friend? Heh. You're hard to figure out sometimes, Zag. But, you know what, we've tried a lot of things. Why don't we try it like you said? I'll see you out there, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I thought you'd never ask. But I'm glad you did.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Um, Meg, I... what I'm trying to say is, are you... are we good, or...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Stop being insecure around me, Zag. You should know better than that by now. Though, yes, if you must know... I think we're good. But if you tell another living soul, or even a dead one, I will kill you, understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... loud and clear, Meg, yes, I understand. So then... what happens now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: What happens now? I'll see you at the edge of Tartarus, I guess. Or maybe here. However long we keep this up.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: However long we keep this up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You ask too many questions, Zag. I don't have the answers, and besides: You know more about living in the moment than I do. See you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...See you around! ...Yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg... what a surprise. Again. What is it? ...What's the matter? Wait. Why are you looking at me like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Shut up already, Zagreus. And come here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I... you really... oh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Be with Megaera? (Either way, it will be OK.)

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I... Meg, I'm deeply flattered, and you're very dear to me, it's just... this isn't what I want from our relationship. I fear I must have led you on. Forgive me. Please say you'll still be my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're sure? Look, I... it's totally all right. I'll see you out there, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I thought you'd never ask. But I'm glad you did.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Um, Meg, I... what I'm trying to say is, are you... are we good, or...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You ask too many questions, Zag. But yes, I'd say we are again, for now. But if you tell another soul, I'll kill you, understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But... no, wait... not even Than...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Than's not an idiot. He wants what's best for you. And he isn't the jealous type. Besides, I have a good working relationship with him, as you well know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, but... what about you, you're the punisher of jealousy. What if I...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not mere mortals, Zag. Mortals cling to one another viciously because their lives are short. What do we care? If Nyx has taught me one thing, it's that the heart has no bounds. Now quit your worrying, and get prepared for when we meet again out there. See you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...See you around! ...Yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos...! I'd ask you to come in, but... you're already here. It's really good to see you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Just tell me one thing, Zagreus. Did you really mean what you told me before, that... maybe we ought to... take our time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Go to Thanatos? (Follow your heart.)

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I did. Sometimes I need to slow things down. I'm thankful that we've been on better terms, lately. But, I've been coming on too strong. Forgive me. You're my dear friend. I don't want to do anything to hurt you. Or anybody else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I... oh. I see. I understand. You're my dear friend, as well. Though we have done an awful lot to jeopardize that lately, haven't we? Look, take care, Zag. Be seeing you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Be seeing you, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I just... don't mean to push you, Than. I know all this is kind of a lot. And I wanted you to know... this isn't some impulsive thing for me. I'll wait for you however long it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Khh! You have no concept of which impulses to act upon, and which to keep in check. You say you'll wait, well, let me ask you this: What are you waiting for? What are you waiting for, I'm here, already. Right...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than...! Hahaha, oh, you're right!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Than, look... speak up, already, I don't like it when you're quiet for too long, what's on your mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: A lot of things. And you will have to grow to like it, Zag. Or, what I mean is... don't take my silence the wrong way, all right? I'd better get going, though, I'm way behind on work, but... see you again. If that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It is. It is.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos...! I'd ask you to come in, but... you're already here. It's really good to see you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Just tell me one thing, Zagreus. Did you really mean what you told me before, that... maybe we ought to... take our time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Go to Thanatos? (Either way, it will be OK.)

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I did. Sometimes I need to slow things down. I'm thankful that we've been on better terms, lately. But, I've been coming on too strong. Forgive me. You're my dear friend. I don't want to do anything to hurt you. Or anybody else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're certain? Well... I understand. I'd best get back to my responsibilities for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...See you, Than.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I just... don't mean to push you, Than. I know all this is kind of a lot. And I wanted you to know... this isn't some impulsive thing for me. I'll wait for you however long it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Khh! You have no concept of which impulses to act upon, and which to keep in check. You say you'll wait, well, let me ask you this: What are you waiting for? What are you waiting for, I'm here, already. Right...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than... hahaha, I'm so glad! It's just... you know that Megaera has been here, too...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. I've known Megaera much longer than you. Who do you think talked me into this?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hah! She did, did she? That's good! That's good.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Than, look... speak up, already, I don't like it when you're quiet for too long, what's on your mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: A lot of things. And you will have to grow to like it, Zag. Or, what I mean is... don't take my silence the wrong way, all right? I'd better get going, though, I'm way behind on work, but... see you again. If that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It is. It is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I spent many days tending this garden... or nights? Quite difficult to tell the time here. But, it's held on longer than I would have expected...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Persephone... I...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus! I missed you so. And dutiful as ever, aren't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm sorry. Know that I am sorry.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Hades... you look tired. Though, I am not the only one you ought to be apologizing to.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I know. I know, I.... Why have you returned? If Olympus... if your mother discovers what happened, not even Zeus, not even he could stop what would transpire, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I've a thought or two about how we can settle that. Though I shall need for you and Zagreus to cooperate with me on a solution. Or, if you'd rather, I could just return the way I came?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: A solution? What, do you intend to have them over for Ambrosia? Not even you can fix a broken family, Persephone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You're right, Hades! I can't. Certainly not by myself. So, are you going to help me, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I would do anything that you would ask of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, first, talk to your son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Mm. Zagreus? You have already met Persephone, our Queen. I expect for you to show deference to her, at all times!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wow, Father, you called me by my name! What is this strange sensation that I feel? Perhaps it's this deference of which you speak?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, stop it, both of you. Don't make me regret this, Hades. Speak honestly with him. About yourself, not me. Go on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes. I... Zagreus. I know there have been times... when I've let my mood reflect on you. I showed very little patience. Questioned your judgment, often, when my own was flawed. Thus, I apologize to you, my son. I have no excuses. And I ask no forgiveness.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What about the part where you lied to me about Mother, do you apologize for that? Your foul moods, and venting them on me... I've long since come to expect that from you. And I resented you for it. But I always thought... you're honest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother did not come here willingly when we originally met. When she left... believing you were stillborn... her decision was final. I believed that she was better off without the lot of us, Olympus included. Deceiving both of you... it tore against my being. But it needed to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, you didn't need to vent all that inner turmoil onto me throughout my life. Though, I think I understand. I only wish I could have understood sooner. So, uh... what now? I've grown so used to ransacking your domain, I think I'm really going to miss it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: About that... your ransacking revealed many flaws in our security. The types that never get into reports. Our reputation rides on there being no escape from here. Would you... keep trying to break free? Not for my sake. But for this realm. For the Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus... If you keep fighting out of here... it shall help keep appearances that everything is as it always was.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Question, Father. Let's say I accept. When next I reach the surface... is it still going to be you waiting up there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, boy. If you think for an instant that I shall go easier on you because of this, you'll soon learn otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So I just battle to the surface and repeatedly kill my own father, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You have it half-correct!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you'll also tend to my little cottage while you're in the area? The garden doesn't take much care at all. I'd like to keep the place in decent shape. In case I cannot suffer this for long.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, well! I guess I'll see what I can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Just do the best you can. And, clearly, I've work to do as well. We have a lot of it ahead of us, I'd say. So, shall we get started, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: After all this time, it's only been a few days! But it's been good. Mother and Father are back together, made some new friends, got this fancy painting! Easy. All is well in the Underworld!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: All is quiet at this time; ever-dreaming Hypnos put a spell upon the House, as willful Zagreus had asked of him. All are fast asleep, save for the Prince. It is exactly as he planned.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Lower your voice, old man, I'm trying to be sneaky here...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: He ventures toward the stately throne of his Lord Father, half-expecting to be caught, but ill-expecting what he is about to find.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I said, shut up, old man!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: He does not know exactly what he seeks. He only knows that something always has felt off to him, that he does not belong. Who is he, really? Lord Hades never would indulge such questions. So, Prince Zagreus would find out for himself.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I'm not listening!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: He rifles through his Lord Father's possessions. There is not much of any import there. Ledgers and administrative parchmentwork. Correspondence from Olympus which he had ignored. No trace of any reference to his son.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: But then, there is the note... written finely in a hand and voice the likes of which the Prince had never seen or heard.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: 'Hades: I can no longer tolerate my life here in this place. So, I am leaving, even if it kills me. I won't be returning to Olympus. If there is a place where I belong in this world, it must be somewhere between heaven and hell. Perhaps it's on the coast and has a little garden. Take care of Cerberus; I shall miss him.'

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Thus did Prince of the Underworld Zagreus absorb the contents of this hidden letter, written in his mother's hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: His mother's hand, my mother's hand, wait, what?! You're saying this Persephone, that she's my... mother...? But, but Father always said that Nyx was my.... That liar. He lied to me, they both did. All my life!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Uhhh erm thus did the Prince discover, inadvertently, the well-kept truth about his lineage. Entirely by chance, this did occur....

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Persephone... the one-time Queen of all the Underworld... where had she gone, and why? Engulfed by newfound questions and his rage, the Prince decided to confront his Lord and Master.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The heavy-sleeping Underworld Prince arises, with the feeling that the brief nap he intended as a respite from the rigors of the day or night apparently was none-too-brief, at all...

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Ungh, I'm late for work... Father's going to kill me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Responsibility-rejecting Zagreus strides down his Father's hall, quite unconcerned about the urgency with which he is expected to behave under a set of circumstances such as this.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Going as fast as I can, you hurry up!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The door to the administrative chamber beckons just beyond the hall. The prince perchance believes that he may enter quietly, without drawing the notice of those laboring within.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: You better not give me away, old man.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The day or night's duties are sheer simplicity itself; at least at first, as when the prince simply signs in to signal the commencement of his shift.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Oh, gods....

endLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
endLines.text: Get on with your responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The often-failing prince attempts to sort the ledgers in the fashion necessary for the proper keeping of his father's realm.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Damn it...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The training-lacking prince's efforts to administer approvals to the judgment terms ultimately leave a lot to be desired.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Ungh...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The hard-laboring shades responsible for the administration of the prince's father's realm merely look on at the prince's ill-fated attempts.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Oh come on.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The relaxation-loving prince never paid suitably enough attention to learn properly how to arrange the record-bookings of the regions of his father's realm.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Doesn't make any sense.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: This set of delicate responsibilities is easily achieved, at least by someone capable and trained to do the work, unlike the oft-distracted Underworld Prince.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: What...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A careful record must be kept of all expenses paid within the House; a delicately-handled tabulation process which the prince is ill-equipped to fully undertake.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I... ungh.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A steadily-updated ledger of the House's income in the form of brilliant gemstones, diamonds, Darkness, and the like, is merely one among the prince's least-liked aspects of his work.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I'll do it later.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Records of expired House Contractor renovations line a desk in the administrative chamber, though the prince has absolutely no desire now to organize them alphabetically, by date.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: None whatsoever, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Attendance records of the working-shades who toil for the House need to be verified; the shades' due compensation, rapidly approved; and yet the prince cannot remember all the necessary steps.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I haven't forgotten, no one told me!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Disorderly Prince Zagreus gazes upon the endless stacks of parchment-paper, each containing work-details to be registered and neatly organized, and starts to feel his optimism fade.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Oh it's faded...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Prince Zagreus must have some favored tasks in the administrative chamber of the House; but surely none of them include the tasks demanding his attention, here.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Correct...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Reviewing lists of claims from shades with grievances is a very important service of the House, or so Lord Hades once attempted to impress upon his willful son.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Can't understand any of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A litany of complaints submitted by restless shades across the Underworld must be evaluated, organized, and processed, but Prince Zagreus does not even know where to begin.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Can't understand any of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Supplies on all the desks in the administrative chamber must be carefully maintained and neatly placed in a specific way, which the forgetful prince has not yet memorized in full.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Eugh...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Security reports concerning failed escape attempts from Tartarus require timely verification and official response, neither of which Prince Zagreus is fit to give.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Eh...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: At last, the work-disliking prince records a summary of the results of his attempts to be of any use to the administrative needs of his grim father's House.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: OK, I'm done, so... can I go yet, Father?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
endLines.text: <Scoffing>

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: It is the dead of night, or the approximation of it in the realm of Hades. Prince of the Underworld Zagreus rises from a fitful slumber, with much mischief on his mind.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Mischief, me? I was just going to have a little look through Father's stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The bedchambers of Prince Zagreus lie in a perpetual state of utter disarray, despite his Lord and master of the House repeatedly insisting that he pick everything up.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Oh come on, it's not that bad, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The ever-energetic Prince alas remains incapable of sitting still, even with such a supple, comfortable seating arrangement nestled in his chambers now.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I just prefer to stand, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: An object intricately carven solely for the purposes of recreation now resides within the pleasure-seeking Prince's chambers, although his lack for an opponent limits its appeal.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Face me yourself, old man.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Much of the softness of the richly woven rug the Prince procured cannot be fully felt beneath his flame-licked feet.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I guess it's soft?

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A massive set of weights positioned carefully in line of sight of any visitors shall doubtless make them think the Prince is stronger and in better shape than in reality.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I get lots of exercise, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The valor of the great-hearted Achilles is immortalized upon the Prince's wall, as though it might stir up more courage from within.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Achilles must have been incredible back when he was alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The visage of the goddess Aphrodite now adorns the Prince's bedchambers, perhaps to provide consolation after when next he dies.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Aphrodite herself. Wonder if I could get this signed.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The ever-smiling, wine-washed countenance of the great Lord Dionysus now adorns the Prince's chamber-wall, thus radiating questionable influence.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: It's like we share a bond, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: With its just-right softness and death-themed embroidery, the new bedding of Prince Zagreus is the envy of all those who wish to rest in peace.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Already bought it, no need to sell me on it, old man.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A delicately crafted instrument of music-making now resides within the clamor-causing Prince's chambers, doubtless fearing, if it could, his heavy-handed touch.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Can't be that hard to play can it?

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The fate-abetting Prince perhaps believes it was through his own choice that he procured the Fated List of Minor Prophecies, but it was preordained.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Oh no, not this determinism thing again...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A beautifully decorated, subtly enchanted bowl containing traces of the hapless Prince's past attempts to flee the Underworld now accents a particularly cluttered corner of his room.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Always wanted my own scrying pool.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The shattered weapons of the siege of Ilion are now reduced to window-dressings in the Prince's ever-cluttered lair.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Bronze weapons... made crudely, but they had the right idea.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The ancient casks of wine hidden within the recesses of the House of Lord Hades... few have tasted the intoxicating vintage they contain, and fewer still are permitted anywhere near their vicinity.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Still waiting for the festive occasion when we'll open one of these.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A commemorative board adorns one of the stoic walls within the lounging area, celebrating and proclaiming the accomplishments of those who serve Lord Hades best of all.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: It's supposed to be updated every so often but it's always Thanatos.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Never made it up there myself!

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Won't ever see my face up on that wall I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The House of Hades: That dark and lavishly appointed lair of the Underworld's King is home not just to him, but to his willful progeny.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: You know I can hear you, old man.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The garden of Persephone the Queen, off-limits to the Prince throughout his life, now is laid bare to him at last in all its splendor.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Not like Mother's cottage, but not bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: What once was a small lounging area within the House of Hades now is sealed off, in utter shambles since the multi-headed Cerberus tore the space apart, mourning the missing Prince.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Cerberus destroyed the lounge again?

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The lounging area within the House of Hades is a dismal place to be, in spite of its intended purpose to enliven the House's grim inhabitants.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: It's better than nothing.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A vast and intricately crafted mosaic depicts the Underworld King presiding over the enormity of his domain, whilst its chthonic residents look on in awe of him.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: He's not really that great.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
endLines.text: Keep it down!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: A majestic gilded edifice of Mount Olympus, crafted with the utmost care from the true mountain's stone, now shines forever brightly even in the dim light of the House of Hades, as a parting gift from the Lord Master's kin.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: The handiwork of Lord Hephaestus, himself! Sounds like he's been rather busy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The courtyard of the House of Hades is kept neat and orderly, in contrast to the terrifying sprawl that lies beyond.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: It's not that orderly really.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Shades of the dead mill constantly within the House, complaining of their woes, and seeking audience from any who would listen.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I listen!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Seldom does the overbearing throne of grim Lord Hades sit unoccupied; thus the House's denizens all go about their business, daring not so much as to ask why.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: That thing doesn't look particularly comfortable.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The frightful countenance of a bleached Bloodless warrior decorates the hall forever more, eternally in tribute to the savage servants of the House's lord.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I could take him.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The dead have utterly no use for sustenance, although they try to feast upon it anyway, as though imagining the customs of the mortal life brings them some sense of peace.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I wish I did not know that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The stoic Lord and Master of the House possesses wealth beyond imagining, a portion of which he has used for a crisp set of attire for every passing day.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I knew it. It's just capes!

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Tsch.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: In his Lord Father's very private chambers, Zagreus, the lock-removing prince, discovers a most delicately painted likeness of none other than Persephone, herself. A coat of dust suggests it has remained here for some time.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: He's never stopped thinking of her. Ugh, what a fool.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The administrative chamber's ever-working shades remain utterly dedicated to their thankless toil all because of an inspiring rendition of how dedicated they ideally should be.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Everybody hang in there!

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Amongst the parchment-records of the dead and punished are the sealed documents known as the Knave-King's Sentence, forcing said king to endlessly toil with a boulder till the end of time.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I'll just patch this through to the House Contractor, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Nestled among the towering administrative parchmentwork of the long-since-deceased lies an old document concerning a special pact signed by a once-living court musician, who attempted vainly to rescue his wife from death.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: OK, it should be dispatched over to the House Contractor now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Buried deep within the archives, holding many binding pacts between Lord Hades and the dead, resides an old agreement; an exchange for services from an extraordinary warrior, for eternity within Elysium for his dear partner.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Should be able to approve a few revisions to that one with the House Contractor I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Within the recesses of the administrative chamber lies the Eldest Sigil of the Master's House: a symbol of the Fate-given authority to rule beneath the earth, and means by which to travel the entirety of all that dark domain.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Could never get the blasted thing to work for me...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The cool, purified waters of the Styx are available in limitless supply to all servants of the god of the dead authorized to work endlessly within the House administrative chamber.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: The job's number one perk... no thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Among the countless splendid gemstones hidden underneath the earth, a great many are used to decorate the House of Hades, god of the dead, god of riches.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I guess there are a lot of gemstones here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Having invested heavily into the cooking-area within the House of Hades, the empty-stomached Zagreus can nonetheless find nothing fit to eat.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: It's all right, wasn't hungry anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The light provided by the flames of deepest hell enhances the lounging area with a soft glow; and their warmth soothes the few living denizens there.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: It's really quite cozy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Water is the source of life, yet still it flows within the darkest Underworld recesses, whilst restless shades of mortals look upon it and recall the short days they could drink it and draw breath.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Now I feel bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Within the House's gallery now sits the bust of a distinguished poet, ruggedly handsome in his age, who, although yet to earn his fated fame, shall probably be better known someday.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Never heard of him.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The gemstone-bearing Prince commissioned such a finely crafted fountain for the House, that all the shades who witnessed it still think of him quite fondly, ever since.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Ever since? It's not been that long.

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: Time seems to stand quite still within the eternal realm of the dead, evidenced even by the means of measuring its passage.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Looks like it's 7:48...

dialogueLines.speaker: Storyteller
dialogueLines.text: The perfect-chiseled physicality of one of Greece's greatest-ever heroes now adorns the House's gallery, reminding most onlookers of many ways in which they fail to compare.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: He sure looks tall...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg. Oh. I was not expecting company. Were you, just, casually snooping, or... is there something I can help you with?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I was just leaving, Zagreus. Happened to be in the area, no thanks to you, and thought I'd go retrieve the last of my possessions. I have to say, you've really let this place go to hell.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...She left. Great. Wonderful! I guess we'll just go back to killing each other repeatedly, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Forget another something in my bedchambers, there, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: No. We need to talk, again. My sisters are involved now, in all this. You've really stirred up quite a mess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry. My mother's out there, somewhere. I won't rest until I can find her. You wouldn't understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You wouldn't know. What I'm trying to say is... with my sisters involved, it changes things. Takes some of the pressure off of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What are you saying?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm saying... I know you need to find your mother, Zagreus. I have my part to play in all of this, but let me see what I can do, if anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg...! She... ah.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Forget another something in my bedchambers, there, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: No. We need to talk, again. My sisters are involved now, in all this. You've really stirred up quite a mess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I know. I'm sorry. But this is something that I have to do. You wouldn't understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You wouldn't know. What I'm trying to say is... with my sisters involved, it changes things. Takes some of the pressure off of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Some of the pressure... wait, what are you going to do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm saying... I know you're doing what you have to do, here, Zagreus. And, my sisters and I will always try to stop you. But I am only doing it because I have to. Understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg...! She... ah.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, you don't have any doors. Why are you always so surprised?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Meg, Than, what... what are you two... what's going on? Whatever it is, I can explain, or... wait, you're both smiling. Oh... let me just... take this in a moment here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You give Thanatos and me too little credit, Zag. We've known each other longer than you've been alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd say we know quite a bit by now, all things considered. Isn't that right, Megaera?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you see we're off duty right now, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Be with Meg and Than? (If you want.)

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Hey... look. I care about you. Both of you. A lot. I'm happy we're this close... you're this close, rather than being at each other's throats like before. But, I think it's all a little much for me, right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're sure? Look... it's totally all right. You've got enough pressure on you as it is. We'll see you out there, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Catch up with you some other time, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Well then... if this turns out to be some sort of dream, I will be very, very mad. Though... I guess there's one good way to find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Whew, well, now, um, where was I...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! I think we've reached an understanding here. I should be off. Until the next one, Zag. And Thanatos.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Whew, well, that was... that was... ahem.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll level with you, pal. You have impressed somebody well above my pay grade here, and so... they have a little proposition for you, you listening?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Let me guess... they want me to fight all the way through the Underworld, having used the Pact of Punishment over there to make the going even more treacherous than it already is, and if I succeed, they'll reward me with some sort of useless trinket?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Wrong! We're talking something really big, here, pal, you see that thing back there? Trinket. Come on, what are you, scared?

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: Yes, a little bit, if you must know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I just knew you had it in you, pal, enjoying your new prize? Pact of Punishment, Shmact of Punishment, no problem, know what I mean? 'Course, that was just the easy one you got there, pretty sure I could've got that one, myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, just so I understand. I literally toiled through hell and back, and my reward is just... a statue of you? Don't get me wrong, it's lovely, it's just... I don't know what I expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, boyo, there was a little mix-up when we put in the request for that thing, see? I told 'em to make something that'll make me look good with my sources, and anyway that's how it all turned out. The other ones turned out much better, though, you'll see!

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I'll temper my expectations just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Look I'm sorry I messed up your statues, boyo. Really. When I saw them, I just kind of freaked. But then I figured there's no way he's going to see how they turned out, so, I just rolled with it, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You shouldn't have underestimated me, mate. But... I shouldn't have said such awful things about your statues. You must have really gone out of your way to get them here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you better believe it, pal! So how about we call it even. We've been through a lot. Both of us! Really learned from this experience, and now, we share a deeper understanding, yeah?

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: We certainly have, Mate. And we certainly do.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly, let me ask you something. Did you really commission three nearly-identical statues of yourself to goad me into using the Pact of Punishment?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I am offended, pal! You haven't even seen the third one yet, so how can you insinuate a thing like that?! Maybe I was wrong about you. I thought you really were the one!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're only saying that to get me to apologize and try and unlock the last of your three identical statues, aren't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: How could you--? I would return those statues for a full refund right here and now, if I could move! And if there was a refund policy on them.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I guess they're stuck here then just like we are.
